transcript_quality: summary only — no call recording on file
address: Birmingham city centre (top-floor apartment with concierge and designated parking; exact postcode not captured)
lead_profile_text: Residential property owner switching to STL. Currently on residential mortgage at £845/month; remortgaging to BTL in October 2026 (expected £500–600/month). Property owned jointly with ex-partner — Harnaik keeping it. Two blockers: (1) short-let clause in lease must be confirmed with building management; (2) HSBC mortgage permission for STL needed before or must wait until October BTL remortgage.
emotional_profile: Engaged and positive — forward-planning, not reactive. Methodical; wants to check the right boxes before proceeding. No explicit objections raised; blockers are open legal/financial questions he needs to resolve.
delay_trigger_phrases: confirm short-let permissions in lease agreement
discuss early-start option with HSBC before October remortgage
delay_timeline: Blocked by lease clause confirmation and HSBC permission; if must wait for BTL remortgage, October 2026 earliest start

top_questions.answer: Must be confirmed with building management or solicitor before proceeding — not confirmed on call

top_questions.text: Will HSBC allow me to operate STL on my current residential mortgage before October?
top_questions.answer: Must be confirmed with HSBC directly — options are explicit consent now or waiting until October BTL remortgage

what_moved_them: York 3-bed case study showing actual figures came within range of projections — provides confidence in Zac's numbers
Concierge and parking surfaced as competitive advantages for the listing (rare in Birmingham city centre)

conversion_gaps.gap: Short-let clause in lease unconfirmed — if prohibited, deal cannot proceed regardless of enthusiasm
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Proactively help Harnaik contact building management or solicitor to confirm; offer to draft the query if Harnaik is unsure who to contact

conversion_gaps.gap: HSBC mortgage permission for STL unconfirmed on current residential mortgage
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Clarify whether explicit consent is possible now or if October BTL remortgage is the only path; do not push for signature until both blockers are resolved

offer_framing: 13%+VAT (reduced from standard) if signed by 22 June 2026. Year-1 adjusted (×0.75) is near-breakeven on current £845 mortgage — real financial story emerges after October remortgage reduces costs to ~£855/month.
